name: Route of the Ermita Virgen de la Peña and
name_original: Ermita Virgen de la Peña y Santuario de la Virgen del Campo — circular desde Camarillas
description: This moderate 16.3-mile mountain biking loop from Camarillas visits the Ermita Virgen de la Peña and Santuario de la Virgen del Campo.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Camarillas offers diverse routes through hilly terrain, river gorges, and pine forests. The region is characterized by its natural landscapes, providing a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents. Riders can explore trails that wind through valleys and past historical hermitages, with varied elevations across the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Camarillas The most popular mountain bike trail is Route of the Ermita Virgen de la Peña and, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and leads past a local hermitage. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Canyon With Waterfall loop from Miravete de la Sierra, a moderate 17.6 miles (28.4 km) path. This route offers scenic views through a canyon, with a notable waterfall feature. Local mountain bikers also love the Villarroya de los Pinares loop from Miravete de la Sierra, a 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Camarillas is defined by hilly terrain, river gorges, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Camarillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Camarillas?
A: There are 9 mountain bike trails around Camarillas listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Camarillas?
A: Yes, Camarillas offers trails suitable for beginners. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39836329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarroya de los Pinares loop from Miravete de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.8 km long and takes about 1 hour 28 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Camarillas?
A: Mountain biking around Camarillas features diverse terrain, including hilly sections, river gorges, and trails winding through pine forests. You'll find a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls in the Camarillas area?
A: Yes, you can experience a notable waterfall feature on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyon With Waterfall loop from Miravete de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt; is a nearby attraction you might want to explore.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Camarillas?
A: Many routes in Camarillas are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyon With Waterfall loop from Miravete de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39836329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarroya de los Pinares loop from Miravete de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Along the trails, you can encounter historical hermitages, such as the one on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005448659/route-of-the-ermita-virgen-de-la-pena-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Ermita Virgen de la Peña and&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarroya de los Pinares&lt;/a&gt; settlement and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2088908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Camarillas?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Just – Gorge of the Río de la Val loop from Aliaga&lt;/a&gt;, covering a distance of 39.6 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Camarillas?
A: Mountain bikers using komoot often praise the varied terrain, which includes hilly landscapes, river gorges, and scenic pine forests. The region offers a good mix of challenging climbs and rewarding descents, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005448659/route-of-the-ermita-virgen-de-la-pena-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Ermita Virgen de la Peña and&lt;/a&gt; being particularly popular for its varied landscape.

Q: Are there any difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there is one difficult-rated trail among the 9 routes available in Camarillas. This trail offers more demanding sections and significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails that go through gorges or canyons?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its river gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyon With Waterfall loop from Miravete de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views through a canyon, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorge of the Río de la Val loop from Aliaga&lt;/a&gt; also explores a notable gorge.

Q: How many people have explored mountain bike trails in Camarillas using komoot?
A: Over 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain and trails around Camarillas, indicating a growing community enjoying the region's offerings.
tips: Beautiful views that we have while we roll through these beautiful roads that give us peace and tranquility near the town of Camarillas.

A charming place near the town of Camarillas. It is the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Campo which is located in the middle of the field right next to the road. We will find a panel that will tell us a little more about the place. There is a fountain in case we want to fill the bottle or refresh ourselves.

Beautiful views that we find from the viewpoint next to the Hermitage of the Virgen de la Peña, patron saint of Aguilar del Alfambra. Remains of a human settlement from 3,900 years ago, from the beginning of the Bronze Age, have also been found. Passage area of the impressive bikepacking route through Alto Alfambra and Sierra del Pobo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ermita Virgen de la Peña y Santuario de la Virgen del Campo mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting and ending in Camarillas, so you can typically find parking within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself doesn't explicitly prohibit dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near religious sites like the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Field and the Viewpoint and Hermitage of Virgen de la Peña. Be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the picturesque landscapes, including almond fields and pine forests, and avoid extreme temperatures, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this circular mountain biking route from Camarillas.

faqs.question: How challenging is this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this mountain biking tour as moderate. While the overall loop is manageable, be prepared for varied terrain that includes some cemented sections with steep slopes and dirt tracks ascending towards the hermitage. Some sections can be physically demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including doubletrack, dirt paths, and several cemented sections, some with steep inclines. The route also involves following a track along a ridge line and descending through ravines.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights of this route are the two religious sites it connects: the Viewpoint and Hermitage of Virgen de la Peña, which is strikingly perched high on a rock, and the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Field. You'll also pass through a Road between crop fields, offering scenic views.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike tour?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking tour typically takes around 2 hours and 15 minutes (8072 seconds) to complete, depending on your pace and stops at points of interest.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I encounter while riding?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque landscapes, including sections that pass through almond fields and pine forests. From the Hermitage of Virgen de la Peña, you'll be treated to expansive views of the infinite plain of Huesca.

faqs.question: Are there any specific gear recommendations for this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including cemented sections with steep slopes and dirt tracks, a mountain bike with good gearing and reliable brakes is essential. We also recommend carrying enough water and snacks, as well as basic repair tools.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular mountain biking route, meaning it starts and ends in Camarillas, allowing you to experience diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Field
